tasks.title: Create artifact template files
tasks.body: Create the following template files under internal/project/templates/artifacts/: adr.md.tmpl (with {{.Title}}, {{.Date}}, {{.Body}} variables, reproducing the strings.Builder output from cmd/adr_create.go:64-75), spec.md.tmpl (with {{.Title}}, {{.Body}} variables, reproducing the fmt.Sprintf patterns from cmd/spec.go:81-86), audit-task.md.tmpl (move existing templates/audits/audit-task.md content, add {{.NodeName}} if needed), task.md.tmpl (with all TaskData fields, reproducing the strings.Builder output from cmd/task/add.go:192-200). Each template must produce byte-for-byte identical output when rendered with the same inputs as the current code.
acceptance_criteria: Each template produces identical output to the current inline string construction
Templates use typed struct field references

tasks.title: Migrate ADR and spec generation to templates
tasks.description: Migrate ADR and spec generation to templates
tasks.body: In cmd/adr_create.go: replace the strings.Builder block (lines 64-75) and os.WriteFile (line 88) with a single RenderToFile call using adr.md.tmpl and ADRData struct. Handle the three branches (stdin body, --body flag, empty template). In cmd/spec.go: replace the fmt.Sprintf branches (lines 81-86) and os.WriteFile (line 87) with RenderToFile using spec.md.tmpl and SpecData struct. The cmd files will need access to a PromptRepository; wire it through the cobra command context or construct one in the Run function.
acceptance_criteria: strings.Builder and fmt.Sprintf file generation code is removed
RenderToFile is used with typed context structs
Output is byte-for-byte identical to previous behavior

tasks.title: Migrate audit-task and task markdown generation to templates
tasks.description: Migrate audit-task and task markdown generation to templates
tasks.body: In internal/project/project_create.go: replace the embedded FS ReadFile + os.WriteFile in WriteAuditTaskMD (lines 40-46) with RenderToFile using audit-task.md.tmpl. The function currently bypasses tierfs entirely; after migration it should resolve through the three-tier system so users can override it. In cmd/task/add.go: replace the strings.Builder in writeTaskMD (lines 192-200) with RenderToFile using task.md.tmpl and TaskData struct. Both call sites currently discard errors (best-effort); preserve that behavior by ignoring the RenderToFile error.
acceptance_criteria: Embedded FS ReadFile is no longer used for audit-task.md
strings.Builder in writeTaskMD is replaced with RenderToFile

tasks.title: Snapshot tests for artifact templates
tasks.body: Write snapshot tests for each artifact template: ADR (with and without body), spec (with body, without body, with stdin), audit-task, and task (with all optional fields, with minimal fields). Each test renders the template with known inputs and asserts the output matches a golden file that captures the current (pre-migration) output. These tests are the primary verification that the migration is behavior-preserving.
acceptance_criteria: Golden file tests cover all artifact templates

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Artifact Templates is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Created adr.md.tmpl, spec.md.tmpl, and task.md.tmpl under internal/project/templates/artifacts/. Each template uses typed struct fields (not map keys) and produces byte-identical output to the original inline string construction. Equivalence verified by 10 table-driven test cases covering body/no-body/multiline/whitespace-only inputs.

task-0001.objective: Create artifact template files that reproduce the inline string construction from cmd/adr_create.go, cmd/spec.go, and cmd/task/add.go
task-0001.what_happened: Created three .tmpl files under internal/project/templates/artifacts/: adr.md.tmpl (Title/Date/Body with conditional default sections), spec.md.tmpl (Title/Body with placeholder fallback), task.md.tmpl (Title with optional Body). Used {{end -}} whitespace trimming to prevent stray trailing newlines from template structure. Wrote table-driven equivalence tests reproducing the original Builder/Sprintf logic and comparing byte-for-byte.
went_well: Template whitespace trimming with {{end -}} cleanly solved the trailing-newline problem without contorting the template layout
Equivalence tests caught the trailing-newline issue during development before it shipped
improvements: The task template relies on the caller to TrimSpace the body before passing it, matching the original code's behavior. This is documented in the test but could surprise a future consumer.
action_items: When call sites migrate to use these templates, the TrimSpace pre-check for task body needs to happen in the render call, not in the template itself
